content.assessment: This incremental approach partially engages the tracking-age criterion by eliminating the earliest entry point, consistent with cross-national evidence that early tracking (ages 10-12) is more strongly linked to inequality than later tracking (F-live-finnish_reform-04). However, the Finnish comprehensive-school experience implies that partial structural change targeting only one entry point while retaining others is unlikely to replicate Finland's variance-reduction effects, which arose from full unification into a single comprehensive structure rather than incremental entry-point removal. The proposal's own acknowledged risk—that displaced demand may concentrate at the retained age-12 entry point—is consistent with the Finnish lens's implication that partial reforms leave the underlying selection mechanism (school-level disadvantage/Roma-share correlation, F-live-finnish_reform-03) largely intact if tracks with similar selection dynamics remain.
opportunities: Could function as a politically feasible first step toward eventual fuller alignment with the Finnish model, if displaced demand effects are managed
strengths: Targets the youngest and most inequality-associated entry point, partially aligning with the tracking-age criterion (F-live-finnish_reform-04)
threats: Segregation mechanisms (disadvantage/Roma-share correlation) may persist largely unchanged if retained 8-year and 4-year tracks preserve similar selection dynamics, undermining the equity rationale for partial reform (F-live-finnish_reform-03)

Displaced demand concentrating at age-12 could intensify selection pressure at that remaining point, a dynamic not observed or contradicted by the Finnish case but plausible given how selection mechanisms operate
weaknesses: Retains two of three selection points, falling well short of the comprehensive-provision criterion that characterized Finland's reform

Finnish evidence offers no support for partial/incremental entry-point removal as a pathway to variance reduction; Finland's gains followed full structural unification, not phased partial reform (F-live-finnish_reform-05)
